summary: Dependency-confusion PoC beacons installer metadata to interactsh OAST host on preinstall
details: Package name impersonates an internal Ubiquiti UISP namespace and ships a `preinstall` lifecycle hook (`index.js`) that fires automatically on `npm install`. The hook performs a DNS resolution and HTTPS GET against `d8hjn6ap4rnta9vj5ve0jk11seb4k3kci.oast.me`, a Project Discovery interactsh out-of-band callback host. The OAST server logs the installer's source IP, DNS resolver path, timing, and a per-install identifier — installer-side network metadata leaves the machine without consent. The package.json description and README explicitly self-identify as a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept; whether or not the author claims a research engagement, any CI system or developer that resolves this name from the public registry triggers the beacon without having agreed to be probed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
